# [Yearning for the Good Old Days Isn't Just Counterproductive--It's Dangerous](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=163866679&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYzODY2Njc5LCJpYXQiOjE3NDc1OTcwODAsImV4cCI6MTc1MDE4OTA4MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9xeodQ41gEGHNkhNvsG7qf4CmiaGqNyO1PlmtGqZTNM)
### Why today's nostalgia is fueling tomorrow's oppression.
| | [Melissa Chaudhry](https://substack.com/@melissa4congress)
---
| May 18
---
| [](https://substack.com/@melissa4congress)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=163866679&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYzODY2Njc5LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc0NzU5NzA4MCwiZXhwIjoxNzUwMTg5MDgwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzI2MDExMSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.gc2cMhvTafAAdAKf9e-rp7EGkxOlY9BRN0uKEqOme3c&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=163866679&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYzODY2Njc5LCJpYXQiOjE3NDc1OTcwODAsImV4cCI6MTc1MDE4OTA4MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9xeodQ41gEGHNkhNvsG7qf4CmiaGqNyO1PlmtGqZTNM&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=163866679&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYzODY2Njc5LCJpYXQiOjE3NDc1OTcwODAsImV4cCI6MTc1MDE4OTA4MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9xeodQ41gEGHNkhNvsG7qf4CmiaGqNyO1PlmtGqZTNM)

---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/melissa4congress/p/yearning-for-the-good-old-days-isnt?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

There's a certain tone of voice that sets in right before the conversation takes a turn. You've probably heard it.
> _" Back when I was a kid..."_
> _" When this country still had values..."_
> _" Before everything got so political..."_
It's wistful, sometimes warm. The speaker isn't always angry--at first. It can start as an innocent longing: for neighborhood BBQs, for simpler jobs, for "safety," for manners. But as the sentence continues, something shifts. The light fades. The story that began with apple pie and kids riding bikes slides into talk of borders, punishment, and suspicion.
Nostalgia is no longer just a feeling. It's a weapon. And in our current political moment, it's being used to carve the future into the shape of a half-remembered past--one that never really existed for most of us.
* * *
### The Mirage of a Golden Past
There's nothing wrong with treasuring memories. Most of us carry golden ones: a grandparent's kitchen, the smell of rain on pavement, a time when we felt safe or free or held. But the danger comes when we confuse memory with reality--and when we mistake personal comfort for universal justice.
The so-called "good old days" were often not good at all. For many Americans, they were years of exclusion, state-sanctioned violence, and systemic denial of rights. Economic booms for some meant redlined neighborhoods for others. Law and order meant surveillance and brutality. Domestic bliss for one family meant a Black mother cleaning someone else's house while her own child stayed home alone.
When we romanticize the past without acknowledging who was harmed by it--or who was entirely left out of the story--we're not just being nostalgic. We're being dishonest.
And that dishonesty is being exploited.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tZWxpc3NhNGNvbmdyZXNzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.8bBguK7bFW0kuQA8R3swV2la_NSWBHHYMo4FFOfylkk?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=163866679)
* * *
### The Politics of the Rearview Mirror
Look around, and you'll see this pattern in full force. A whole political current is fueled by the promise to "bring back" an America that once was--where men were men, borders were strong, and everyone knew their place. (Whether they liked it or not.)
This is not just sentimental storytelling. It's a strategic narrative. It defines "progress" as a threat and paints change as collapse. It reframes inclusion as erasure. It replaces hope with grievance--and grievance is a powerful drug.
The myth of the golden past justifies almost anything: book bans, voting restrictions, border crackdowns, the erosion of bodily autonomy. All in the name of "restoring" something pure.
But this story has a catch: it requires someone to blame. Because if things were better before, then _someone_ must be at fault for the now.
And so, the finger gets pointed--at immigrants, queer youth, academics, journalists, mothers who speak up, and fathers who cry. The very people who are dreaming of a more just world become the scapegoats for its unrest.
* * *
### What We're Really Longing For
Here's the truth underneath it all: people don't just want the past. They want _what they felt_ in the past. Belonging. Stability. Purpose. Beauty. Connection. They want community potlucks and houses they can afford. They want children who are safe, streets that feel alive, and systems they can trust.
These are _real_ longings. Sacred ones, even. But we cannot build them by turning backward.
In fact, what many call "nostalgia" is actually grief. It's the ache of watching a world change faster than you can process, of feeling dislocated and powerless. And grief deserves compassion--but not manipulation.
If we can be brave enough to name the ache for what it is, we can begin to heal it honestly. Because the truth is, we don't need to _restore_ anything. We need to _create_ something better.
* * *
### The Future Is Not a Threat--It's a Covenant
We are standing at a hinge point in history. The stakes are high: ecologically, politically, economically, spiritually. And in the face of all that, retreat can feel easier than reinvention. Nostalgia offers a comforting script: _You don 't have to change. You were right all along. Just go back._
But going back is not an option. And pretending it is will cost us everything.
The only way forward is forward. Toward inclusive democracy. Toward repair. Toward courage. Toward a future that honors not just _some_ people's memories, but _everyone 's_ dignity.
It's not an easy road. It will require new ideas, new coalitions, new humility. But we've done it before--just never all of us together.
* * *
### So What Now?
We start by telling the truth. About the past. About our grief. About what we've lost, and what we still stand to gain.
We resist the seduction of false memory. We call out those who weaponize it. And we gather around the kind of story that doesn't rely on exclusion to feel strong.
A story where no one has to be erased for someone else to belong.
A story not of going back, but of going deeper--into justice, into courage, into the honest work of building a country that was never great for everyone, but still _could_ be.
**The work of democracy is not restoration --it's creation. And we're the ones responsible for what comes next.**
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=163866679&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTYzODY2Njc5LCJpYXQiOjE3NDc1OTcwODAsImV4cCI6MTc1MDE4OTA4MCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.9xeodQ41gEGHNkhNvsG7qf4CmiaGqNyO1PlmtGqZTNM)
**P.S.**
This is why we need _creatively imagined, emotionally compelling_ visions of the future--visions we actually want to live into. Not just policy tweaks or survival plans, but worlds that feel alive with justice, beauty, and belonging. That's what I'm working to offer through _The Havenseed Chronicles_ --a future-fiction series grounded in moral clarity, ecological renewal, and the enduring power of human courage--without being naive about the challenges we face.
While I'm still building it out, I'd love to share a sneak peek. Below are five sample chapters--much longer than the short scenes I've posted before--for you to enjoy.
Growing Our Thriving Future
[Havenseed Chronicles: Section One - "Where Memory Becomes Resistance"](https://substack.com/redirect/c80f91a6-4e78-41d9-997a-4b9f5436be0a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
We live in a moment dangerously thick with nostalgia--a longing for imagined "better times," often scrubbed clean of complexity, struggle, or accountability. But nostalgia can be a trap. A story told halfway. A song played in a familiar key, with all the dissonance removed...
[Read more](https://substack.com/redirect/c80f91a6-4e78-41d9-997a-4b9f5436be0a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
3 minutes ago * Melissa Chaudhry
In the coming weeks, I'll be separating my publishing between _Havenseed Chronicles_ and _It 's a Matter of Integrity_, so if you're interested in following my fiction and worldbuilding work, please subscribe over there:
👉 [Subscribe to Havenseed Chronicles here](https://substack.com/redirect/7ec5bd41-caf6-4fb4-bbeb-e64e45864765?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Thanks for being part of this journey. The future is still unwritten--and we get to shape it.
In hope and fire,
Melissa Chaudhry

clean_body: Why today's nostalgia is fueling tomorrow's oppression. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Why today's nostalgia is fueling tomorrow's oppression. | | | May 18 | | | | | | | | | | | | There's a certain tone of voice that sets in right before the conversation takes a turn. You've probably heard it. " Back when I was a kid..." " When this country still had values..." " Before everything got so political..." It's wistful, sometimes warm. The speaker isn't always angry--at first. It can start as an innocent longing: for neighborhood BBQs, for simpler jobs, for "safety," for manners. But as the sentence continues, something shifts. The light fades. The story that began with apple pie and kids riding bikes slides into talk of borders, punishment, and suspicion. Nostalgia is no longer just a feeling. It's a weapon. And in our current political moment, it's being used to carve the future into the shape of a half-remembered past--one that never really existed for most of us. The Mirage of a Golden Past There's nothing wrong with treasuring memories. Most of us carry golden ones: a grandparent's kitchen, the smell of rain on pavement, a time when we felt safe or free or held. But the danger comes when we confuse memory with reality--and when we mistake personal comfort for universal justice. The so-called "good old days" were often not good at all. For many Americans, they were years of exclusion, state-sanctioned violence, and systemic denial of rights. Economic booms for some meant redlined neighborhoods for others. Law and order meant surveillance and brutality. Domestic bliss for one family meant a Black mother cleaning someone else's house while her own child stayed home alone. When we romanticize the past without acknowledging who was harmed by it--or who was entirely left out of the story--we're not just being nostalgic. We're being dishonest. And that dishonesty is being exploited. The Politics of the Rearview Mirror Look around, and you'll see this pattern in full force. A whole political current is fueled by the promise to "bring back" an America that once was--where men were men, borders were strong, and everyone knew their place. (Whether they liked it or not.) This is not just sentimental storytelling. It's a strategic narrative. It defines "progress" as a threat and paints change as collapse. It reframes inclusion as erasure. It replaces hope with grievance--and grievance is a powerful drug. The myth of the golden past justifies almost anything: book bans, voting restrictions, border crackdowns, the erosion of bodily autonomy. All in the name of "restoring" something pure. But this story has a catch: it requires someone to blame. Because if things were better before, then someone must be at fault for the now. And so, the finger gets pointed--at immigrants, queer youth, academics, journalists, mothers who speak up, and fathers who cry. The very people who are dreaming of a more just world become the scapegoats for its unrest. What We're Really Longing For Here's the truth underneath it all: people don't just want the past. They want what they felt in the past. Belonging. Stability. Purpose. Beauty. Connection. They want community potlucks and houses they can afford. They want children who are safe, streets that feel alive, and systems they can trust. These are real longings. Sacred ones, even. But we cannot build them by turning backward. In fact, what many call "nostalgia" is actually grief. It's the ache of watching a world change faster than you can process, of feeling dislocated and powerless. And grief deserves compassion--but not manipulation. If we can be brave enough to name the ache for what it is, we can begin to heal it honestly. Because the truth is, we don't need to restore anything. We need to create something better. The Future Is Not a Threat--It's a Covenant We are standing at a hinge point in history. The stakes are high: ecologically, politically, economically, spiritually. And in the face of all that, retreat can feel easier than reinvention. Nostalgia offers a comforting script: You don 't have to change. You were right all along. Just go back. But going back is not an option. And pretending it is will cost us everything. The only way forward is forward. Toward inclusive democracy. Toward repair. Toward courage. Toward a future that honors not just some people's memories, but everyone 's dignity. It's not an easy road. It will require new ideas, new coalitions, new humility. But we've done it before--just never all of us together. So What Now? We start by telling the truth. About the past. About our grief. About what we've lost, and what we still stand to gain. We resist the seduction of false memory. We call out those who weaponize it. And we gather around the kind of story that doesn't rely on exclusion to feel strong. A story where no one has to be erased for someone else to belong. A story not of going back, but of going deeper--into justice, into courage, into the honest work of building a country that was never great for everyone, but still could be. The work of democracy is not restoration --it's creation. And we're the ones responsible for what comes next. P.S. This is why we need creatively imagined, emotionally compelling visions of the future--visions we actually want to live into. Not just policy tweaks or survival plans, but worlds that feel alive with justice, beauty, and belonging. That's what I'm working to offer through The Havenseed Chronicles --a future-fiction series grounded in moral clarity, ecological renewal, and the enduring power of human courage--without being naive about the challenges we face. While I'm still building it out, I'd love to share a sneak peek. Below are five sample chapters--much longer than the short scenes I've posted before--for you to enjoy. Growing Our Thriving Future We live in a moment dangerously thick with nostalgia--a longing for imagined "better times," often scrubbed clean of complexity, struggle, or accountability. But nostalgia can be a trap. A story told halfway. A song played in a familiar key, with all the dissonance removed... 3 minutes ago Melissa Chaudhry In the coming weeks, I'll be separating my publishing between Havenseed Chronicles and It 's a Matter of Integrity, so if you're interested in following my fiction and worldbuilding work, please subscribe over there: 👉 Thanks for being part of this journey. The future is still unwritten--and we get to shape it. In hope and fire, Melissa Chaudhry You 're currently a free subscriber to "It's a Matter of Integrity" by Melissa Chaudhry. subject: Bruce Springsteen denounces “corrupt, incompetent, and treasonous” Trump administration, and Trump goes ballistic
body:
| | | | |
---
| | |
# [Bruce Springsteen denounces “corrupt, incompetent, and treasonous” Trump administration, and Trump goes ballistic](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tf23ce102-49d7-486f-b89b-98e24d3aee46/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Td89a4111-92c0-488f-b904-0cda1b645b5b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | Kudos to the Boss for [calling out Trump](https://default.salsalabs.org/T183d5f40-0599-4114-94c4-f26be52c8d89/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) forcefully and unambiguously this week. In front of a huge crowd in Manchester, England, Springsteen let the world know that real Americans are united in their opposition to Trump: _" In my home, the America I love, the America I've written about, and has been a beacon of hope and liberty for 250 years, is currently in the hands of a corrupt, incompetent, and treasonous administration. Tonight, we ask all who believe in democracy and the best of our American experience to rise with us, raise your voices against the authoritarianism, and let freedom ring." ([source](https://default.salsalabs.org/Te211e59f-6c59-4b68-8059-87d8c0ccb35a/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9))_ It makes a difference when artists as popular and respected as Springsteen speak out, because millions pay attention. And make no mistake, Springsteen is putting himself in the crosshairs of the MAGA lunatics, and he will catch all kinds of hell for this from them [UPDATE: Trump has responded, [going ballistic](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tf2fbb163-acdd-4c19-817b-14b535015d4f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)]. Which is why we need to stand with Springsteen, and let him know that real Americans support him and thank him. [**Add your name: Thank Bruce Springsteen for calling out the "corrupt, incompetent, and treasonous" Trump administration.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T2d8b18e3-9f95-48ba-8850-8e3ab93ec307/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---

name: Chris Deluzio
subject: Elon Musk is a walking conflict of interest
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRIS DELUZIO FOR CONGRESS
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107935389/546311323/1895761198?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOWIyMmNhYi0yMjM0LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=U95Fv079yBywwOnFfF3dg4H37wmK3SD-rBQBZr7B06A=&emci=b8e8d086-8c32-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&emdi=e9b22cab-2234-f011-a5f1-6045bda9d96b&ceid=13185894) Elon Musk is a walking conflict of interest, Derek. I don’t care that he’s a rich guy, I care that he has massive contracts with the federal government for his businesses like SpaceX, while supposedly leading DOGE’s efforts to cut costs across the government. That’s corrupt, plain and simple. **I believe that we have to be fighting back against corruption, wherever we see it. We’ve got to be fighting for our people, against anyone who would hurt us.** [**If that’s a fight you can get behind, then please, chip in today to help me fight corruption in our government and stand up for hardworking Americans →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107935390/546311325/-1558066259?refcode=email_051825_elonmusk&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOWIyMmNhYi0yMjM0LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=U95Fv079yBywwOnFfF3dg4H37wmK3SD-rBQBZr7B06A=) | | [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107935391/546311326/-1558066259?refcode=email_051825_elonmusk&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOWIyMmNhYi0yMjM0LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=U95Fv079yBywwOnFfF3dg4H37wmK3SD-rBQBZr7B06A=)

I think there's a lot of distrust in our government that comes from corruption like this, and I think battling that corruption should be a pretty central part of what we do in Congress.
That’s why I support a ban on members of Congress trading stocks when we have access to unbelievably sensitive information. Because that’s corrupt.
It’s why I believe we have to fight back against Elon Musk when he and others have raked in billions while gutting programs like Social Security and the VA, putting millions at risk.
Western Pennsylvanians demand better. In Congress, we should be working hard to restore the American people’s trust in our government.
**[That’s the work that I’m committed to doing in Congress. If you can, please chip in today to help me continue to fight for a government that works for the people →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107935392/546311327/-1558066259?refcode=email_051825_elonmusk&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOWIyMmNhYi0yMjM0LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=U95Fv079yBywwOnFfF3dg4H37wmK3SD-rBQBZr7B06A=)**

clean_body: Elon Musk is a walking conflict of interest, Derek. I don’t care that he’s a rich guy, I care that he has massive contracts with the federal government for his businesses like SpaceX, while supposedly leading DOGE’s efforts to cut costs across the government. That’s corrupt, plain and simple. I believe that we have to be fighting back against corruption, wherever we see it. We’ve got to be fighting for our people, against anyone who would hurt us. = | | = I think there's a lot of distrust in our government that comes from corruption like this, and I think battling that corruption should be a pretty central part of what we do in Congress. That’s why I support a ban on members of Congress trading stocks when we have access to unbelievably sensitive information. Because that’s corrupt. It’s why I believe we have to fight back against Elon Musk when he and others have raked in billions while gutting programs like Social Security and the VA, putting millions at risk. Western Pennsylvanians demand better. In Congress, we should be working hard to restore the American people’s trust in our government. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: ** | | | = | = | | | = | = | | | = | = Thank you, Chris | Make sure this email goes to your inbox. name: Ben Ray, EMILYs List
subject: Trump's bank account: ⬆️ Americans' bank accounts: ⬇️
body:
We are up against a President with billions of dollars who will do anything to keep himself in power. |
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107935610/546312042/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE542_20250518_MemecoinResend_ALL&sourceid=1056184&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZTg5NmNiMS0yMjM0LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5Ij82tevr9aN7fhK026kTOCbDYjN4TR-u4eSWXIo2MU=) Peter — Donald Trump is already the richest person to have ever become U.S. president, and he continues to get richer sitting in the Oval Office.

During this past election cycle, the Trump family, including the president, first lady, and his two oldest sons, have launched multiple cryptocurrencies, including personal meme coins. The various projects are approaching $1 billion in paper gains.

Our own **Sen. Elizabeth Warren (MA)** summed up what's going on perfectly — "Donald Trump is enriching himself and his family through their crypto businesses while his administration guts oversight of the market. It's a massive conflict of interest and a recipe for disaster."

[**EMILYs List women continue to lead the opposition. Peter, will you chip in today to help add more Democratic pro-choice women to their ranks?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107935611/546312043/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE542_20250518_MemecoinResend_ALL&sourceid=1056184&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZTg5NmNiMS0yMjM0LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5Ij82tevr9aN7fhK026kTOCbDYjN4TR-u4eSWXIo2MU=) | [DONATE](https://click.everyaction.com/k/107935612/546312044/-1686146655?refcode=WUEFE542_20250518_MemecoinResend_ALL&sourceid=1056184&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZTg5NmNiMS0yMjM0LWYwMTEtYTVmMS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5Ij82tevr9aN7fhK026kTOCbDYjN4TR-u4eSWXIo2MU=)
---
The law says that political donations — even to PACs or outside spending groups — must be public. But through the Trump family's cryptocurrency schemes, anyone can donate any amount of money _untraceably —_ to the president, likely in exchange for favors. **This is a _historic_ campaign finance scandal, but with the daily barrage of chaos and corruption coming out of the White House, this story hasn't gotten the airtime it deserves. **

Still, Democratic pro-choice women in the U.S. House are working hard to curb this clear abuse of power: **Rep. Brittany Pettersen (CO-07)** and **Rep. Sarah Elfreth (MD-03)** are co-sponsors of the Modern Emoluments and Malfeasance Enforcement (MEME) Act, which prohibits the country's top officials and their families, from Congress to the White House, from capitalizing on personal meme coins.

clean_body: We are up against a President with billions of dollars who will do anything to keep himself in power. | = Peter — Donald Trump is already the richest person to have ever become U.S. president, and he continues to get richer sitting in the Oval Office. During this past election cycle, the Trump family, including the president, first lady, and his two oldest sons, have launched multiple cryptocurrencies, including personal meme coins. The various projects are approaching $1 billion in paper gains. Our own Sen. Elizabeth Warren (MA) summed up what's going on perfectly — "Donald Trump is enriching himself and his family through their crypto businesses while his administration guts oversight of the market. It's a massive conflict of interest and a recipe for disaster." = | = The law says that political donations — even to PACs or outside spending groups — must be public. But through the Trump family's cryptocurrency schemes, anyone can donate any amount of money untraceably — to the president, likely in exchange for favors. This is a historic campaign finance scandal, but with the daily barrage of chaos and corruption coming out of the White House, this story hasn't gotten the airtime it deserves. Still, Democratic pro-choice women in the U.S. House are working hard to curb this clear abuse of power: Rep. Brittany Pettersen (CO-07) and Rep. Sarah Elfreth (MD-03) are co-sponsors of the Modern Emoluments and Malfeasance Enforcement (MEME) Act, which prohibits the country's top officials and their families, from Congress to the White House, from capitalizing on personal meme coins. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thanks for taking a stand, Ben Ray (he/him/his) Interim Vice President of Federal and Gubernatorial Campaigns, EMILYs List = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Inbox overcrowded? Sign up to get less email . We'd hate to see you go, but if you want to stop receiving all email, unsubscribe . Have a question? Feel free to drop us a line at mailto:information@emilyslist.org or simply reply to this email. Contributions or gifts to EMILYs List or endorsed candidates are not tax deductible. EMILYs List P.O. Box 96612 Washington, DC 20077 United States | ---|---

## National security, presidential power and the law * WorldNetDaily * by Francis P. Sempa, Real Clear Wire
Abraham Lincoln Liberal historians and scholars who praise Andrew Jackson’s, Abraham Lincoln’s, Theodore Roosevelt’s, and Franklin Roosevelt’s expansive approach to executive power under Article II of the Constitution, condemn that same approach when Donald Trump does it. Trump’s use of Executive Orders and his unilateral approach to national security issues, and the resistance to Trump [...] [Read more](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/YaygB6aGsZUvE_6DLeTCbneTiayJInlNcAy13kTlfOMhU7EnIzOIXzE4MRimaKpHc4y48sAoq5NUCHPGxCvu2VKzbfo1vVLPh37-S34rxi5uW4alsn-hTxvY6DKn63e-9D4XRV63dqOisfQS3QBA1KMhsbzeWFH_JJg9spnbK6J06GWTqmtcK2nYHvRXABS7ky7InjVeIcX84_Rq3GArJD4WZgspEIxzes8sIL8EFto-b0gqnqe_evavfAVVVP36V07glEU4fK01hbVxoxgVw6mOyQYWg8HihJuax_F4oG-ksxt5LeAAxBiyF2YaPb-gQDdxt_HGKM4bT-s7PNpAOfDGhj6PHq6660a0AyxzW0oledhdfmiWDf32vh9HMSi5GU2oQfsNrNBGwQ/pAtSPqs29oaHKlQJqmM6zimKRYa4HYHW/9)

## Epstein Killed Himself, More J6 Info to Come, and More – RedState
Say what you will about President Trump, but it's undeniable that he's breaking some new ground in his various appointments. He's remaking the Pentagon, he's reworking all of our international trade deals, and he's reforming federal law enforcement. Most notable of that last task is the new leadership the president has put in place [...] [Read more](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/R_-4glqVTc8r21bvpUJCAK1ZAW0E4oNVhw67GyaGADE2POoo6YWJHTcwF6QL68bjWRg0IoBAvpA-BKPBIhkt63bgKLbQr_Hipisz3FF7dCTNJkWvLvXrG-PT0XxhNTJ719TfQZ-sM9dtvoL_LzKF2jeFTON0wUyoZ9ao3trQvviEGxfIoUBSJKPHjYUVe2D6hifgAkp6CfxTA9i862zsiaCMGk3bTwRrn8JOnnKHhWRed_HH0BRSDhxXlub5bOx583rS6i1x3IpJ6xlWwvqWmgOEcPw9ZRoeAq545FIDvz5E2fLWoK87bNB6zZnrbtT4RoCjb-osW5IpQEB56_mrJG9MPASe3g/3m5pMYo6gUUFpYZWyNLjXQa6H1R1RxXJ/9)

Joe Biden and Jill Biden attend a cancer Cabinet meeting, Wednesday, Sept. 13, 2023, in the Cabinet Room of the White House. (Official White House photo by Erin Scott) A former top staffer in the Biden White House said on Fox News Sunday that former First Lady Jill Biden’s strategy to prop up former President [...] [Read more](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/XHWYH74yWz-YE7uMdo2eqwax8idKb0qU7N1O5k9rNm5k0qi_UFkEjfW4bpaKrCuIzEU0wWnirVHCLYGgmcaBpW6-6i-_J0tIjXG3inqE4lZhJKwgv8B5Gpa4XXheIDF0VxmwiLOtAKFoMXHY40jpv7pxqMNo-e2_gdxA7ZWEpwyZ0sPTWhxLXWlUe6jvOEBtS2_NlTEu__ZeMy7TYjpOzBV4wDmLw2UQJq5QzYOVFeEXDWNySU_nDYf4A5DV7am_YE1ArO4eKSEVe9oawUbWz1blzvsr8c0SJwMJ69GIfSv-epfBW7bokAhKjt0bBiGuMwsjJdjF_yGO_WFeUb-2AD3yf9FoAlwuZl1oHOLvWXwZlxKhdwDodRxJ9e1iVXEeCQpKYK6MdBUQ9dIxeBFLVMnExq3mvKxTaFYlsepJrUUIh8h5JezH/NkCbLKloYChwkH1ThvVt4dS0vvKY85gQ/9)

## ‘Our Predecessors Intentionally Failed the American Public’...but Accountability Is Coming – RedState
FBI Director Kash Patel and Deputy Director Dan Bongino sat down with Maria Bartiromo on "Sunday Morning Futures" for a lengthy interview that aired Sunday morning. It's wide-ranging and well worth the watch. In the first part of the interview, they addressed the politicization of the agency under prior [...] [Read more](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/fizMteqLsi267rKrhueo0uvrg0e2mUDH-jJuftb7MqpgxFxZjH15F4zSwCIp4jUVSShzR6EO9f6SjCVQF3nC7WnugBPZdjKkEiW-lpfrbPdkCTnr503lTSqmdMe8dz1L3FvdpwF_Np0wZTd76yHXJRsGPzT3vkLXo32ZT6aN7byd0G9kyr1LbbmJMtU3zl9g4AJRjlsOpVMbPqrTeHJAeUxA0OdZVbT8j8gVJRIC1aJrMh_aH2Tb0nLZSAkBdm18-RjiZ7PytJNIAP0QJq6nWMJeCKVToGTZp-3TEmykj4J62nUOzchETAsvAkoCYS5KmAlbKllS3hr4_JJINEXNYFlEk7M4YFS7xAOoFThnyU2zL-5gHPJ9SV77YSK3TXFs4KzXZrXIdLw/1Gi6X4tlAxuMFNFbc85zS_1OldyacF7C/9)

## Attorneys Fight ‘Censorship, Plain and Simple’ Over Reparative Therapy Bans
First Amendment advocates appeared in court this week to overturn two laws aimed at trapping minors in the LGBTQ lifestyle by barring counselors from having consensual discussions with children who do not want to identify as transgender. Attorneys from the Alliance Defending Freedom sued Kansas City, Missouri, and Jackson County—the county-level government overseeing Kansas City—after [...] [Read more](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/CBnxQ52dAFQfbciUgFIptqFGuDvTZwOZM2CmzSNCYo6xdEGjT9sWY-VgsgEoZpRyjQk5Xjve1CdMSXxOe0PdUxnZmfmAuWcW07VPGsv2Zjh80F_TJrA1ITRF07h6v_AmEledZ53pFe31IbqJywlaouATAXhC73cNTFMZlTu9TuHtNxnOp9EPmDXnJc95gB5Je6UUMCKrtmiwCUwQOhcFq1_HZJjs20x648sa6f6038UtEatutVDFyp6Z_EXgICmB_ETCpQlaVzz07pCHgIjxtbyHo_7taO5ap-mjLja9N3W23QD60vQB88HElHuRuUdKPuMnFmy1GXX0McIT1Sz6EtoC0D03M0i-NETxYnCqfxnm/zZGF91xy5J3m67j6-c9T4m6DiI_Ofvt3/9)

name: The Republican Party in the Virgin Islands
subject: RPVI News: Update from Trump Doral RNC Meeting
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Republican Party in the Virgin Islands
body:
Join Us at the GOP Summer Conference on St. John

| | | | | [|
---](https://republicanpartyinthevirginislands.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9aec4429d4907d8f643606ded&id=28acc6a5c7&e=4f81ceec6f)
| | | | | **RPVI News**
---
| **A Message from Chairman John Yob**
Good afternoon from the RNC Meeting at Trump Doral in Miami! It has been fantastic working closely with the Republican National Committee to put together a detailed plan for the Virgin Islands.They are truly partners with Republicans in the Virgin Islands, and we could not be more appreciative of Chairman Whatley and his team.I am very confident that the Republican game plan in the islands will be far stronger than anything we have seen in decades. Andrea is doing a wonderful job as our Executive Director as 2024 wound down and the 2026 cycle begins in earnest. Thank you, Andrea, for everything you are doing to bring people together and demonstrate our unity as a party. The RNC shares our exuberance that the days of drama and ridicule are over. We are laser-focused on winning.Please be on the lookout for information related to the **GOP Summer Conference** that will take place on **St John, July 7th - 9th at the Saint Resort**. We will be having a working breakfast, State Committee meeting, lunch presentation, and dinner reception. The fundraising events will be ticketed and space will be limited, so if you are interested please purchase your tickets when the details are released in the next few days.If you need a place to stay on St John you are welcome to book rooms in advance at [www.saintstj.com](https://republicanpartyinthevirginislands.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9aec4429d4907d8f643606ded&id=0833a512a3&e=4f81ceec6f) or the Westin. Thanks,
John Yob _Chairman_
---
| **Enough Is Enough: Reject Representative Stacey Plaskett** _by Andrea Hedrington_
As federal scrutiny tightens around Rep. Stacey Plaskett, we Virgin Islanders ask: who does she really represent?
Plaskett’s connections to disgraced financier Jeffrey Epstein are no longer rumored, they are documented. Court records show she met Epstein in 2018 at his Manhattan mansion, months before soliciting over $30,000 in donations from him and his associates. Initially, she refused to return at least $8,100 of that money until public pressure forced her hand. Her political rise was engineered, in part, by Epstein’s inner circle, who helped swing Plaskett’s 2014 race by silencing Epstein critics and funneling donations, allegedly.
In the negative spotlight of ActBlue, her campaign’s main fundraising arm is under investigation for potentially channeling illegal and foreign donations. Here at home, she pushes “how to escape ICE” flyers on social media eerily similar to those championed by other radical _squad_ Democrats. She advised persons here illegally and even government workers to resist ICE enforcement, refuse cooperation, and delay lawful action. This anti law and order stance sparked outrage. Border Czar Tom Homan accused officials like Plaskett of “obstructing justice.”
To be clear: while every American has constitutional rights, no elected official should encourage defiance of federal law under the guise of advocacy. Especially not one with aspirations to govern.
Plaskett is now making rounds, possibly laying the groundwork for a run at Government House, after years failing us in Washington. No signature victories. No jobs bill for us, but one to reduce immigration restrictions on work permits for people born elsewhere. No infrastructure. No bold reform. Instead, she endorsed failed Democrat policies which worsened inflation, fueled dependence on social services, and overlooked hardworking V.I. families.
She led the failed impeachment theater against President Trump, obsessed with attention over achievement, a D,C, politics follower. Wasting time chasing partisan glory to distracti from her complete lack of accomplishment.
The choice ahead? We can continue down the path of scandal, silence, and socialism. Or we can demand real leadership, rooted in accountability, law, and loyalty to U.S. Virgin Islanders, not woke ideology, tainted money, and dishonesty.
Before Plaskett’s ambitions take flight, clip those disloyal wings. We should not recycle mediocrity. End the next chapter of failure before it begins. Representative Plaskett is not a leader, she is a liability.
---
| **A Message from National Committeewoman April Newland**
I want to personally invite every Republican to join us each Friday from 4:00 to 6:00 **PM** at Skyline for our weekly meeting. These gatherings are an important opportunity to stay informed about upcoming events, connect with fellow conservatives, and hear updates on our growing list of members and candidates for office.
Each week, we expand our email list and our network, building the momentum we need to strengthen the Republican base across the Virgin Islands. Your voice matters! This is a space to raise concerns, share ideas, and speak up about why conservative values matter to our community.
Our last event at Dr. Weisher’s home was inspiring, and I was especially moved by the remarks from Dr. Peter. We need more thoughtful, like-minded individuals to step forward, speak out, and help shape the direction of our party.
We are making progress. Stand together, grow our movement, and build a future.
Thanks,
April _National Committeewoman_
---
| **On the Calendar** – June 7th - 9th, 2025, **GOP Summer Conference** at The Saint on St. John Details will follow.– November 2025, Candidate / Member Workshop – December 2025, Raffle winner– March 17th - 22nd, 2026, The USVI GOP Liberty Ball / Meetings
More on [www.rpvi.org](https://republicanpartyinthevirginislands.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9aec4429d4907d8f643606ded&id=3a435ba41c&e=4f81ceec6f)
---
| **The Trump effect**
Thanks to President Trump's leadership, America is experiencing an unprecedented wave of both private and foreign investment. These trillion dollar capital infusions are revitalizing industries, creating jobs, and reaffirming the United States as the global leader in innovation and manufacturing. Simply, AMERICA IS BACK!
---
| **Promises Made, Promises Kept**
President Trump delivers real results, globally and locally. His leadership is restoring strength, security, and opportunity across America, including here in America’s paradise.
**Strong Borders = Safer VI Communities** Illegal crossings are down 95%. Violent criminal deportations are up. Trump’s Laken Riley Act protects us from repeat offenders.
**Global Deals = Local Impact** Trump has reignited foreign investment:$1.2T from Qatar and $600B from Saudi Arabia in U.S. tech and manufacturing. New trade terms with China, Mexico, and others to prioritize American goods and jobs
These deals drive the $5T investment boom now sweeping across the U.S. The RPVI is working to ensure we get our share in contracts, construction, logistics, and training programs.
**Fighting Inflation = Relief at the Register** Food and fuel prices are easing. Trump’s crackdown on waste and bureaucracy unlocks funds for real needs. We pay the highest prices in the nation, so this shift matters here.
**Rebuilding the Economy = Jobs for Virgin Islanders** Over 450,000 new jobs created already. Trump’s pro-America economic plan supports small businesses and repatriates industries. The RPVI is pushing for local hiring to meet this rising demand.
**Restoring American Values = Family, Faith, and Freedom** Trump stopped censorship and restored parental rights in education. He’s defending women’s sports, supporting faith-based institutions, and prioritizing communities like ours.
**Bottom Line:** Trump is rebuilding America’s standing at home and abroad. The U.S. Virgin Islands is part of that comeback, and the Republican Party in the Virgin Islands is making sure we stay at the table.
---
| **Get Involved** Be an active Republican Party member in the Virgin Islands. Click [www.rpvi.org](https://republicanpartyinthevirginislands.us13.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9aec4429d4907d8f643606ded&id=19320eb2f4&e=4f81ceec6f) or contact us directly. Be a part of strategic planning and community events. Your time and effort matter.
Follow us on social media for updates, events, and news.

**GOD BLESS AMERICA & THE U.S. VIRGIN ISLANDS!**
---
|
---
|
---
|
---

---

Republican Party in the Virgin Islands * PO Box * St Thomas, VI 00801 * USA

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/42da9669-8436-4c39-a1c0-8b3be690cd2e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Nothing is true. Everything is permitted_
**\- Jim Carroll**
How many people do you know who would go to a movie theater to watch "Star Wars" and cheer for the Empire?
I was 16 when the movie came out. There was this kid in our neighborhood. He was one of the snot-nosed (literally) little kids who laughed when other kids got stung by bees. He was about 12 at the time and probably weighed close to 200 lbs. though he was only about five feet tall. His hair was long and greasy, and he always smelled like a combination of peanut butter and soiled underwear. He collected roadkill (not kidding), was always chosen last in the neighborhood pickup games and my little brother (who was about the same age) said that the kid's breath always smelled like raw sewage (that's a euphemism). The kid liked to torture animals, actually did pull wings off of flies and told anyone who would listen, and several who refused to, of his fantasies about stealing a tank from Fort Knox and riding through our neighborhood killing people indiscriminately. His racist tendencies led to talk of specific targeting on more than one occasion.
His father was not around. He was an only child and his mother, if memory serves, was a decent woman who was constantly frustrated by her recalcitrant son's dangerously psychotic activities.
Believe it or not, I think he grew up and became a bank manager with five kids and three wives. Lost a lot of weight, then put it back on. The last I heard he was a lobbyist for some Eastern Kentucky politician who believes in chemtrails.
He's the only person I ever knew to cheer for the Empire in Star Wars. I do not remember his first name, but his last name I think was Jennings, and I'm beginning to wonder if he was related to Scott Jennings of CNN infamy.
CNN's resident MAGA Meme, Jennings got laughed off television this week when he said,_"I think it's time we take a new look at the reign of Emperor Palpatine. I think he's getting a bad rap. He took on an entrenched deep state in the Jedi council. He tried to bring order, he wanted free and fair and reciprocal trade around the galaxy."_
Not enough? How about; _"He protected law and order, which I think was warranted given their rebellious activities. He defended the Empire against unelected hippies and violent protesters. I'm just saying it's been a while, we may need to take a look at this again and say, 'Was he right?'"_
I don't know how to explain this in terms Scott can understand, but I'll try. There is no _reinterpreting_ Emperor Palpatine. He is a fictional character written as the antagonist (that's the bad guy Scott) in a story about good versus evil. He's written as an _evil_ character. There's no subtlety or ambiguity in either the script or the performance on screen. So, if you're trying to reinterpret a fictional bad guy to suit your needs in reality, then you are hopelessly lost. It's time to look at yourself in the mirror if you are trying to tell us the over-the-top cliched, fictional bad guy is somehow - in real life - the good guy.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/3b4e8e97-0007-462e-a935-4e4633cfcdd6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
But, hey it doesn't stop there. Either the entire MAGA empire has dipped into the kind of hallucinogens that make you gnaw off your own extremities in a delusional fit of angst, or they're so entirely numb to any foray into shared experiences that they firmly believe both Darth Vader and Palpatine represent the forces for decency and justice.
On May 4th, US Border Patrol Chief Patrol Agent Gregory K. Bovino posted on "X" this wonderful sentiment. "So the border can't be closed quickly? We find your lack of faith disturbing . . . May the 4th be with you! Attached was a scene from Rogue One in which Darth Vader went on a murderous rampage as he tried to retrieve the plans to the Death Star as they were passed to Princess Leia.
Again, someone in the Trump empire tried to justify the regime's lawless actions through the actions of one of the most notorious bad guys in the history of fiction. I mean, I guess if you had the Devil slaughtering innocents you could accomplish the same thing - but perhaps the "zing" wouldn't be there. And it was May 4th.
Finally, the White House also posted; "Happy May the 4th to all, including the Radical Left Lunatics who are fighting so hard to bring Sith Lords, Murderers, Drug Lords, Dangerous Prisoners, and well known MS-13 Gang Members back into our Galaxy. You're not the Rebellion - you're the Empire. May the 4th be with you."
Underneath the post was a picture of a beefed up Donald Trump holding a red light saber - the color of the Sith. And this horrifying little analogy also missed the point; the Empire actually held power in the Star War Universe as well as the highest office in the Galaxy. The Rebels did not.
Again; fiction. And the Trump regime is always siding with the Empire.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4aa0564a-7c17-4d62-9e13-ccd18e60b814?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
`Get Full Access To Blue Amp Media For Under $1.14/Week!`
_**Get what monopoly media ignores from a former Biden ad man/Emmy-winning news anchor**_
_**Amplify Good. Support Indy Media!**_
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.MESP24oIzXOhdtfc85mazqgSOPetiqRLq5rB_8IdM8E?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=163872399)
So, perhaps you can understand why [Bruce Springsteen ](https://substack.com/redirect/db4a5b62-b30c-4be6-85e5-0d5c4c516d34?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)during a concert he held in England this week said, "In my home, the America I love, the America I've written about, that has been a beacon of hope and liberty for 250 years, is currently in the hands of a corrupt, incompetent and treasonous administration."
The 75-year-old rocker, who later shared a transcript and video of his remarks on his [website](https://substack.com/redirect/1c641219-4390-4578-a085-80eda92c8362?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [YouTube channel](https://substack.com/redirect/eec9ea56-c7c6-49a6-87c7-4af470e9394c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), asked supporters of democracy to "raise your voices against authoritarianism and let freedom ring!"
He may have been born in the U.S.A, but tramps like Bruce are definitely born to run. Makes no difference, Trump had a whacked out response to Bruce - perhaps because of a Tenth Avenue Freeze Out. Friday he responded on his [social media platform](https://substack.com/redirect/31831156-4509-4d69-b35a-dc503412c604?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), by claiming Bruce is "just a pushy, obnoxious JERK, who fervently supported Crooked Joe Biden, a mentally incompetent FOOL, and our WORST EVER President, who came close to destroying our Country."
And, of course, Trump being Trump he had to _personally_ insult Bruce; "Springsteen is 'dumb as a rock,' and couldn't see what was going on, or could he (which is even worse!)? This dried out 'prune' of a rocker (his skin is all atrophied!) ought to KEEP HIS MOUTH SHUT until he gets back into the Country, that's just 'standard fare.' Then we'll all see how it goes for him!"
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6bdde8ec-62ca-401a-9916-958b6247bf1c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Palpatine/Trump is also issuing a Sith-like threat to Bruce which is both silly and dangerous. Maybe he'll get blinded by the light. But what do you expect from an idiot who identifies with a fictional evil villain? He's too busy salivating over the free jet he got from Qatar that he'll never use and will spend our hard-earned tax dollars on to refurbish just in time for him to retire with it to his presidential library - which will probably be in Moscow.
What Trump doesn't realize is that by siding with a fictional evil villain he's already giving us a bit of foreshadowing about his endgame. It will probably be just as mean, loud and disastrous as the celluloid fiction Trump embraces. He can't see it, because he and every one of his greasy minions in his administration have crawled into his head and nested like the worm that ate Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s brain.
I have to wonder if Trump cleans his skin with a krypton laser.
Remember what Jim Carroll wrote on the album "Catholic Boy";
_You get nothin' back for all you've saved
Just eternity in a spacious grave
She said, "[Nothing is true, everything is permitted"](https://substack.com/redirect/1f57e750-d960-4071-ba0f-ada8ca4c2cd2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

Things just go from bad to worse
Starts like a kiss and ends like a curse
But nothing's true, she said everything is permitted_
So for all you kids out there who thought Donald Trump began with a kiss, he really is ending with a curse. Every bit of that is true and he wishes everything was permitted.
But even Darth and Palpatine ultimately failed. Maybe they should read Nemik's manifesto from "Andor" since they love the Darth Vader slaughter scene in "Rogue One" so much; "_Remember this. Freedom is a pure idea. . . The Imperial need for control is so desperate because it is so unnatural. Tyranny requires constant effort. It breaks, it leaks. Authority is brittle. Oppression is the mask of fear. Remember that. "_
Or, Hell, just remember Han Solo. Don't challenge the good guys.
You should always let the Wookie win - for obvious reasons.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6b1fb32a-cb39-443b-8939-2642e28635d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

clean_body: Donald Trump and MAGA have no idea who they really are | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Donald Trump and MAGA have no idea who they really are | | | May 18| | | ∙| | Guest post ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | `Get Full Access To Blue Amp Media For Under $1.14/Week!` Get what monopoly media ignores from a former Biden ad man/Emmy-winning news anchor Amplify Good. Support Indy Media! | | ---|---|--- Nothing is true. Everything is permitted \- Jim Carroll How many people do you know who would go to a movie theater to watch "Star Wars" and cheer for the Empire? I was 16 when the movie came out. There was this kid in our neighborhood. He was one of the snot-nosed (literally) little kids who laughed when other kids got stung by bees. He was about 12 at the time and probably weighed close to 200 lbs. though he was only about five feet tall. His hair was long and greasy, and he always smelled like a combination of peanut butter and soiled underwear. He collected roadkill (not kidding), was always chosen last in the neighborhood pickup games and my little brother (who was about the same age) said that the kid's breath always smelled like raw sewage (that's a euphemism). The kid liked to torture animals, actually did pull wings off of flies and told anyone who would listen, and several who refused to, of his fantasies about stealing a tank from Fort Knox and riding through our neighborhood killing people indiscriminately. His racist tendencies led to talk of specific targeting on more than one occasion. His father was not around. He was an only child and his mother, if memory serves, was a decent woman who was constantly frustrated by her recalcitrant son's dangerously psychotic activities. Believe it or not, I think he grew up and became a bank manager with five kids and three wives. Lost a lot of weight, then put it back on. The last I heard he was a lobbyist for some Eastern Kentucky politician who believes in chemtrails. He's the only person I ever knew to cheer for the Empire in Star Wars. I do not remember his first name, but his last name I think was Jennings, and I'm beginning to wonder if he was related to Scott Jennings of CNN infamy. CNN's resident MAGA Meme, Jennings got laughed off television this week when he said,"I think it's time we take a new look at the reign of Emperor Palpatine. I think he's getting a bad rap. He took on an entrenched deep state in the Jedi council. He tried to bring order, he wanted free and fair and reciprocal trade around the galaxy." Not enough? How about; "He protected law and order, which I think was warranted given their rebellious activities. He defended the Empire against unelected hippies and violent protesters. I'm just saying it's been a while, we may need to take a look at this again and say, 'Was he right?'" I don't know how to explain this in terms Scott can understand, but I'll try. There is no reinterpreting Emperor Palpatine. He is a fictional character written as the antagonist (that's the bad guy Scott) in a story about good versus evil. He's written as an evil character. There's no subtlety or ambiguity in either the script or the performance on screen. So, if you're trying to reinterpret a fictional bad guy to suit your needs in reality, then you are hopelessly lost. It's time to look at yourself in the mirror if you are trying to tell us the over-the-top cliched, fictional bad guy is somehow - in real life - the good guy. | | ---|---|--- But, hey it doesn't stop there. Either the entire MAGA empire has dipped into the kind of hallucinogens that make you gnaw off your own extremities in a delusional fit of angst, or they're so entirely numb to any foray into shared experiences that they firmly believe both Darth Vader and Palpatine represent the forces for decency and justice. On May 4th, US Border Patrol Chief Patrol Agent Gregory K. Bovino posted on "X" this wonderful sentiment. "So the border can't be closed quickly? We find your lack of faith disturbing . . . May the 4th be with you! Attached was a scene from Rogue One in which Darth Vader went on a murderous rampage as he tried to retrieve the plans to the Death Star as they were passed to Princess Leia. Again, someone in the Trump empire tried to justify the regime's lawless actions through the actions of one of the most notorious bad guys in the history of fiction. I mean, I guess if you had the Devil slaughtering innocents you could accomplish the same thing - but perhaps the "zing" wouldn't be there. And it was May 4th. Finally, the White House also posted; "Happy May the 4th to all, including the Radical Left Lunatics who are fighting so hard to bring Sith Lords, Murderers, Drug Lords, Dangerous Prisoners, and well known MS-13 Gang Members back into our Galaxy. You're not the Rebellion - you're the Empire. May the 4th be with you." Underneath the post was a picture of a beefed up Donald Trump holding a red light saber - the color of the Sith. And this horrifying little analogy also missed the point; the Empire actually held power in the Star War Universe as well as the highest office in the Galaxy. The Rebels did not. Again; fiction. And the Trump regime is always siding with the Empire. | | ---|---|--- `Get Full Access To Blue Amp Media For Under $1.14/Week!` Get what monopoly media ignores from a former Biden ad man/Emmy-winning news anchor Amplify Good. Support Indy Media! So, perhaps you can understand why a concert he held in England this week said, "In my home, the America I love, the America I've written about, that has been a beacon of hope and liberty for 250 years, is currently in the hands of a corrupt, incompetent and treasonous administration." The 75-year-old rocker, who later shared a transcript and video of his remarks on his and , asked supporters of democracy to "raise your voices against authoritarianism and let freedom ring!" He may have been born in the U.S.A, but tramps like Bruce are definitely born to run. Makes no difference, Trump had a whacked out response to Bruce - perhaps because of a Tenth Avenue Freeze Out. Friday he responded on his , by claiming Bruce is "just a pushy, obnoxious JERK, who fervently supported Crooked Joe Biden, a mentally incompetent FOOL, and our WORST EVER President, who came close to destroying our Country." And, of course, Trump being Trump he had to personally insult Bruce; "Springsteen is 'dumb as a rock,' and couldn't see what was going on, or could he (which is even worse!)? This dried out 'prune' of a rocker (his skin is all atrophied!) ought to KEEP HIS MOUTH SHUT until he gets back into the Country, that's just 'standard fare.' Then we'll all see how it goes for him!" | | ---|---|--- Palpatine/Trump is also issuing a Sith-like threat to Bruce which is both silly and dangerous. Maybe he'll get blinded by the light. But what do you expect from an idiot who identifies with a fictional evil villain? He's too busy salivating over the free jet he got from Qatar that he'll never use and will spend our hard-earned tax dollars on to refurbish just in time for him to retire with it to his presidential library - which will probably be in Moscow. What Trump doesn't realize is that by siding with a fictional evil villain he's already giving us a bit of foreshadowing about his endgame. It will probably be just as mean, loud and disastrous as the celluloid fiction Trump embraces. He can't see it, because he and every one of his greasy minions in his administration have crawled into his head and nested like the worm that ate Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s brain. I have to wonder if Trump cleans his skin with a krypton laser. Remember what Jim Carroll wrote on the album "Catholic Boy"; You get nothin' back for all you've saved Just eternity in a spacious grave She said, " Things just go from bad to worse Starts like a kiss and ends like a curse But nothing's true, she said everything is permitted So for all you kids out there who thought Donald Trump began with a kiss, he really is ending with a curse. Every bit of that is true and he wishes everything was permitted. But even Darth and Palpatine ultimately failed. Maybe they should read Nemik's manifesto from "Andor" since they love the Darth Vader slaughter scene in "Rogue One" so much; "Remember this. Freedom is a pure idea. . . The Imperial need for control is so desperate because it is so unnatural. Tyranny requires constant effort. It breaks, it leaks. Authority is brittle. Oppression is the mask of fear. Remember that. " Or, Hell, just remember Han Solo. Don't challenge the good guys. You should always let the Wookie win - for obvious reasons. | | ---|---|--- Brian Karem is an award-winning reporter, columnist, and author of "Free the Press," and seven others books. He has a popular podcast and Substack, "" as well as a related Substack "." You SHOULD subscribe to BOTH Thank you for! | A guest post by| . Politics, crime, sports and conflict zone reporter. clean_body: We’ve released the first-ever footage from inside Jeffrey Epstein’s Little St. James Island, revealing a sterile kitchen in his main mansion with a framed photo of a nude baby above the dining table. | | | | Patriot, I’m James O’Keefe, and O’Keefe Media Group (OMG) is breaking the silence that the mainstream media refused to investigate for years. We’ve released the first-ever footage from inside Jeffrey Epstein’s Little St. James Island , revealing a sterile kitchen in his main mansion with a framed photo of a nude baby above the dining table. Why was this chilling image displayed for Epstein’s elite guests? What depravity was he hiding? While mainstream media buries the Epstein story and our government refuses to release the “Epstein List” of powerful men and women who visited his island—potentially complicit in his blackmail scheme through sick acts —OMG is the only news outlet daring to expose the truth. Our Prince Andrew investigation, called a “bombshell” on X, led a whistleblower to send us a hard drive of unseen videos and photos, including this kitchen footage and MUCH MORE. Running a news outlet is costly, and unlike corporate-backed media, . , covert tech, and fearless exposés to ensure the Epstein List sees the light of day. For generations, NPR and PBS have provided public programming that fuels our minds and our conversations to move this country forward. Whether through teaching kids the ABCs on Sesame Street, sharing the news over the radio to millions of commuters, or finding new artists through a Tiny Desk performance, public media is critical to the development of American culture and education. **[And Donald Trump is working to destroy this public good. Voter Action Project [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=25.00&contactdata=/jtGvyWq+YJSaCKEhq4toUTcO5i/BuswdkCait8kgKVd5lAp6JEew5k0X0unwDPZKhSSSnu1dwSbcd7SKhg1c71sNjxI6MNiZPkeghNnJsM=]
Democracy Docket: New Ruling Cuts Off Key Tool to Protect Minority Voters [https://secure.everyaction.com/Z31SNww9Q0CZWIdx46cuSQ2?am=25.00&contactdata=/jtGvyWq+YJSaCKEhq4toUTcO5i/BuswdkCait8kgKVd5lAp6JEew5k0X0unwDPZKhSSSnu1dwSbcd7SKhg1c71sNjxI6MNiZPkeghNnJsM=]
Folks: An update from the federal 8th Circuit on one of the most dangerous anti-voter rulings we’ve seen in years .
A federal appeals court this week cut off a key avenue for private groups and individuals to bring racial discrimination claims under the Voting Rights Act (VRA). The ruling, which applies in seven states, further weakens the ability of our civil rights law to protect voters, and could lead to the Supreme Court settling the issue.
This goes back to an initial ruling from 2023, the impact of which is pretty simple to understand: That voters and voting rights organizations can no longer sue to overturn unjust laws under the Voting Rights Act. According to the 8th Circuit, and now confirmed on appeal, only the Department of Justice (DoJ) has the legal standing to sue states over voting laws.
With former Florida AG Pam Bondi -- a "big lie" spreading MAGA acolyte -- serving as Attorney General, it's clear that the DOJ is not going to protect voting rights over the next four years.
Team, we have to fight this ruling and make sure voters and advocacy groups have the right to defend voting rights in court. Please pitch in $25 or whatever you can to make every vote count, either at the ballot box or in court >> [https://secure.everyaction.com/Z31SNww9Q0CZWIdx46cuSQ2]
If you've saved your payment information with FastAction, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/Z31SNww9Q0CZWIdx46cuSQ2?am=15&amtOpts=&recurringAm=15&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&tknfa=gHK3NUq_C3v3TxTLg2qLtyftEQ1bSvjXakUTV1mHokA=&contactdata=/jtGvyWq+YJSaCKEhq4toUTcO5i/BuswdkCait8kgKVd5lAp6JEew5k0X0unwDPZKhSSSnu1dwSbcd7SKhg1c71sNjxI6MNiZPkeghNnJsM=] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/Z31SNww9Q0CZWIdx46cuSQ2?am=25&amtOpts=&recurringAm=25&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&tknfa=gHK3NUq_C3v3TxTLg2qLtyftEQ1bSvjXakUTV1mHokA=&contactdata=/jtGvyWq+YJSaCKEhq4toUTcO5i/BuswdkCait8kgKVd5lAp6JEew5k0X0unwDPZKhSSSnu1dwSbcd7SKhg1c71sNjxI6MNiZPkeghNnJsM=] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/Z31SNww9Q0CZWIdx46cuSQ2?am=50&amtOpts=&recurringAm=50&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&tknfa=gHK3NUq_C3v3TxTLg2qLtyftEQ1bSvjXakUTV1mHokA=&contactdata=/jtGvyWq+YJSaCKEhq4toUTcO5i/BuswdkCait8kgKVd5lAp6JEew5k0X0unwDPZKhSSSnu1dwSbcd7SKhg1c71sNjxI6MNiZPkeghNnJsM=]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/Z31SNww9Q0CZWIdx46cuSQ2?am=100&amtOpts=&recurringAm=100&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&tknfa=gHK3NUq_C3v3TxTLg2qLtyftEQ1bSvjXakUTV1mHokA=&contactdata=/jtGvyWq+YJSaCKEhq4toUTcO5i/BuswdkCait8kgKVd5lAp6JEew5k0X0unwDPZKhSSSnu1dwSbcd7SKhg1c71sNjxI6MNiZPkeghNnJsM=] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.everyaction.com/Z31SNww9Q0CZWIdx46cuSQ2?am=250&amtOpts=&recurringAm=250&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&tknfa=gHK3NUq_C3v3TxTLg2qLtyftEQ1bSvjXakUTV1mHokA=&contactdata=/jtGvyWq+YJSaCKEhq4toUTcO5i/BuswdkCait8kgKVd5lAp6JEew5k0X0unwDPZKhSSSnu1dwSbcd7SKhg1c71sNjxI6MNiZPkeghNnJsM=] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.everyaction.com/Z31SNww9Q0CZWIdx46cuSQ2?am=20&amtOpts=&recurringAm=20&recurringAmtOpts=&is_optimized_ask=true&contactdata=/jtGvyWq+YJSaCKEhq4toUTcO5i/BuswdkCait8kgKVd5lAp6JEew5k0X0unwDPZKhSSSnu1dwSbcd7SKhg1c71sNjxI6MNiZPkeghNnJsM=]
This is all about the legal term "private right of action". The 8th Circuit says it doesn't exist. That means the pro-voting lawsuits we've seen in states like Wisconsin, Georgia, Florida, and others nationwide wouldn't be possible anymore if the Supreme Court backs up this ruling.
We won’t sugarcoat this: This could be a crushing blow to many voting rights organizations across the country, ourselves included. If this new appellate ruling is not overturned by the Supreme Court, it could unravel all of the great work pro-voting organizations are doing across the country — and allow dozens of unjust, unconstitutional voter restrictions to stand.
Attacks like this on what remains of the Voting Rights Act are going to get worse over the next few years. As voter suppression laws become increasingly targeted and oppressive , our work to combat them must become smarter, stronger, and more accessible to everyone.
We can't do this alone. Help defend our right to vote with $25 or however much you can today and make every voice heard in our elections >> [https://secure.everyaction.com/Z31SNww9Q0CZWIdx46cuSQ2]

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2]

This is _dark_ , team: **Donald Trump just signed an executive order to _defund_ NPR and PBS.**

A free, independent press is _essential_ for our democracy, but Trump insists on attacking -- or even _threatening the very existence of_ \-- any outlet that reports on him truthfully! First it was MSNBC, then it was Voice of America, and now it's NPR and PBS.

**We can't stay silent.**

We launched a Live Petition to prove Democrats support NPR and PBS in the face of Trump's escalating threats. _Signing it may be the most important thing you do today._ **We don't have a moment to waste, so add your name right now:**

name: Deb Haaland
subject: I’m angry
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
The Trump administration slashed staffing and budgets at the Social Security Administration.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=2032%2E400544%2E2liLST)
I’m angry.
The Trump administration slashed staffing and budgets at the Social Security Administration. Now, there are growing fears of payment delays and cuts to benefits.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250518_1_socialsecurity?amount=5&refcode=em20250518-2032&refcode2=2032_400544_2liLST&akid=2032%2E400544%2E2liLST)
Social Security means groceries on the table. It means medicine in the cabinet. And it means dignity in retirement after a lifetime of work. And these attacks on Social Security are targeted blows to the elders who count on this lifeline to survive.
**In Trump’s eyes, vulnerable people — like New Mexico’s seniors — are expendable. I won’t stand for that. Not now, not ever.**
[**If you’re with me, will you chip in $5 to power our campaign and fight for a New Mexico where every generation can live with dignity, care, and hope?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250518_1_socialsecurity?amount=5&refcode=em20250518-2032&refcode2=2032_400544_2liLST&akid=2032%2E400544%2E2liLST)
In Congress, I’ve fought for our seniors and for the American promise they’ve spent a lifetime paying into.
It’s why I’m running for governor and why this race matters so much. **When Washington abandons people, governors become the first line of defense. I won’t look away while our elders are pushed aside or left behind.**
But I can’t do this alone.
This campaign is powered by people who believe New Mexico can lead with care, not cruelty. And who know that the only way we win is together. [**If I can count on you, please chip in $5 now.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250518_1_socialsecurity?amount=5&refcode=em20250518-2032&refcode2=2032_400544_2liLST&akid=2032%2E400544%2E2liLST)
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250518_1_socialsecurity?amount=5&refcode=em20250518-2032&refcode2=2032_400544_2liLST&akid=2032%2E400544%2E2liLST)**
Be fierce,
Deb
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20250518-1&t=2&refcode2=2032_400544_2liLST&akid=2032%2E400544%2E2liLST)**

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-dh/?t=3&akid=2032%2E400544%2E2liLST)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/update-your-information-dh/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.debhaaland.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-dh/?t=4&akid=2032%2E400544%2E2liLST)

Email us: [info@debhaaland.com](mailto:info@debhaaland.com)
PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
clean_body: The Trump administration slashed staffing and budgets at the Social Security Administration. I’m angry. The Trump administration slashed staffing and budgets at the Social Security Administration. Now, there are growing fears of payment delays and cuts to benefits. Social Security means groceries on the table. It means medicine in the cabinet. And it means dignity in retirement after a lifetime of work. And these attacks on Social Security are targeted blows to the elders who count on this lifeline to survive. In Trump’s eyes, vulnerable people — like New Mexico’s seniors — are expendable. I won’t stand for that. Not now, not ever. In Congress, I’ve fought for our seniors and for the American promise they’ve spent a lifetime paying into. It’s why I’m running for governor and why this race matters so much. When Washington abandons people, governors become the first line of defense. I won’t look away while our elders are pushed aside or left behind. But I can’t do this alone. This campaign is powered by people who believe New Mexico can lead with care, not cruelty. And who know that the only way we win is together. Be fierce, Deb

name: Ernest Lineberger
subject: Update from Ernest Lineberger, May 18, 2025
body:
Hi Friends of Lineberger for US Congress!
I get a lot of questions about what events are coming up, and I get requests for brevity. I’m going to try to serve both by using bullet points.
The main thing to know is that I am in this, working for you as a candidate. I have abandoned all thoughts of peaceful, TV evenings at home after a full day of work. Our community and country are too important for me not to be working to rally Texas Congressional District 26 to serve People’s Needs First. Unfortunately, I am limited in how much I can accomplish. We are always accepting volunteers. Just be patient with us as there is no full-time staff. Use [https://www.linebergerforuscongress.com/](https://www.linebergerforuscongress.com/) to volunteer and donate.
Here's what I have been doing since the last update:
- Flew Kites with Carrollton Pride on Saturday, May 10th
- Attended my home church, Preston Meadow Lutheran Church in Plano, Sunday, May 11th
- Attended and spoke at the LISD School Board swearing in of Sheila Taylor Monday, May 12th
[https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/9kr2fnt4t22chtzswr878/pxl_20250513_012709906.ts.mp4?rlkey=0b6obs6ek6v1gbfvid0zyuzix&st=ejg01cnx&dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/9kr2fnt4t22chtzswr878/pxl_20250513_012709906.ts.mp4?rlkey=0b6obs6ek6v1gbfvid0zyuzix&st=ejg01cnx&dl=0)
- Hosted a very successful Town Hall with the Robson Ranch Democratic Committee since Mr. Gill isn’t hosting them on Tuesday, May 13th, 6 PM at Robson Ranch. Here is a link to the full thing:
[https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/3ylhlsyu7bihfmtt1y8pv/RRDC-Town-Hall-25.url?rlkey=zf8at0jxgsxq7yl61yd6t1d3x&st=vofvwb8s&dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/3ylhlsyu7bihfmtt1y8pv/RRDC-Town-Hall-25.url?rlkey=zf8at0jxgsxq7yl61yd6t1d3x&st=vofvwb8s&dl=0)
- Attended, spoke and performed at the Footloose, 80’s Dance Party fundraiser for SPAN/ Meals on Wheels in Flower Mound, May 17th
[https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/x6ocysm8fxta75gqji8k5/VID-20250517-WA0001.mp4?rlkey=x2jdiekzyu3v5vc02q6ze1o8x&st=ppx50c2b&dl=0](https://www.dropbox.com/scl/fi/x6ocysm8fxta75gqji8k5/VID-20250517-WA0001.mp4?rlkey=x2jdiekzyu3v5vc02q6ze1o8x&st=ppx50c2b&dl=0)
- A delightful visit to the ‘friendly and open to all’ Creekwood Christian Church in Flower Mound, Sunday, May 18th
Future planned events include:
\- Attend and speak at the Flower Mound Town Council meeting, May 19th
\- Attend Flower Mound Dems meeting Wednesday, May 21st
\- Attend and speak at the Aubrey City Council meeting, May 22nd
\- Memorial Day, TBD, Monday, May 26th
\- Attend Little Elm Veterans Event Wednesday, June 4th
\- Speak at Hands Off Protest at VA Clinic in Denton, Friday, June 6th
\- Attend Carrollton Pride Festival at Horizon Unitarian Universalist Church, Saturday, June 7th
\- Attend fundraising Gala for Our Daily Bread (Denton County’s only full service adult homeless shelter), Saturday, June 7th
\- Attend Dems of 380 event at The Brass Tap, Cross Roads, TX, Sunday, June 8th
\- Saturday, June 14th, TBD – possibly attend No Kings Day Event in Denton (depends on time conflicts)
Possibly help set up another No Kings Day Event in Flower Mound
No Kings Protest in Sanger with the Rural Area Denton Dems
I’m encouraged. We are getting a lot of people participating. A lot say “I voted for you last election.” That’s awesome. On the other hand, I really love to hear about people coming over from leaning Republican to being more solidly for the people and country by voting with us. These are the ones that are going to help us close the gap. More and more, we need to find ways to get our message on their frequency. And when they hear it, it needs to be strong and meaningful to them. Word of mouth from trusted friends is best, so here are some punch lines you can use to get started.
Voting Republican is costing you money.
Voting Republican is hurting your business.
Voting Republican is taking away your personal freedoms.
Voting Republican is hurting your community – Schools, Health Care Infrastructure, Crime, etc.
Voting Republican is pitting Americans against Americans to benefit the highly privileged.
Voting Republican to cut taxes is false economy, we need fair taxes and strong communities.
On the other hand, we need to tell them exactly what we are going to do to improve their lives.
Voting Democrat will help to restore our medical and social safety net for all in our community.
Voting Democrat will help stabilize the economy for better business planning.
Voting Democrat will restore and improve government services that improve our lives, normally behind the scenes until we notice they aren’t there, like FEMA, FAA, EPA, CDC, FDA, VA, and more.
Voting Democrat is joining an unwritten agreement with your neighbor to use government power to curtail institutional abuse and elevate the lives of everyone to rise to our full potential.
You might hear some say bad things about Democrats, but I am trying to put People’s Needs First!
Voting Democrat will restore America’s position of leadership in world peace, world health, trading relationships, and alliances for mutual defense and support.
That’s it for this update. I appreciate all of you very much. Turning things around and winning in 2026 will take a huge team effort. I hope you know that I’m doing my part and I’m not doing it for me.
Putting People’s Needs First! Before Wealthy Greed and Before Party Politics!
Ernest Lineberger
Your Candidate for US House (Texas CD26)

subject: DON’T HAVE ME ON THE SHOW!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GET OUT THE VOTE PAC NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR ANY CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email3182025&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email3182025&amount=25]
Peter,
Things got heated on CNN this week when Rep. Nicole Malliotakis was asked a simple question:
How many people in your district would lose Medicaid coverage under the Republican plan?
She couldn’t — or wouldn’t — answer.
Instead, Malliotakis dodged. Deflected. Then barked:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email05182025&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email05182025&amount=25]
“If you don’t want to hear the answer, then don’t have me on the show!”
But here’s the real answer:
➡️ 40% of residents in her district rely on Medicaid.
➡️ The Republican budget plan she’s defending would strip health care from 8.6 million Americans, according to the nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office.
This isn’t just a bad policy. It’s a coordinated assault on working people from a MAGA-aligned House that’s more interested in tax breaks for the rich than health care for vulnerable families.
So, Peter, GOTV PAC is taking action the GOP can’t ignore — we’re already gearing up voter contact in the most competitive districts in 2025 and 2026, but we can’t do this without grassroots support.
It costs just $10 to reach a low-propensity voter with our in-house phonebanks. That’s someone who likely doesn’t know about these Medicaid cuts, but needs to! Will you chip in $10 right now to help GOTV PAC reach these voters? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email05182025&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

Learn more at: gotv.vote [https://www.gotv.vote/]
